fact: February 22 is exactly 53 days from the start of the year - that's 7 weeks and 4 days of winter madness!
In the Northern Hemisphere, maple trees are secretly getting ready to explode with sap right around this time - some trees can produce 10 gallons of sap per day!
Why do cats get extra crazy in late February? Their winter fur is so thick by now that they're basically walking around in a giant fuzzy sleeping bag!
February 22 falls smack in the middle of Pisces season - the fish zodiac sign that's supposed to make people extra dreamy and weird.
In Antarctica, this date marks the peak of summer, and penguin chicks are getting so fat and fluffy they look like walking cotton balls with beaks!
The sun is climbing higher in the sky every day now - in northern cities, you gain about 3 minutes of daylight each day around February 22!
Gross but cool: earthworms start wiggling closer to the surface around this time because the ground is beginning to thaw, making them easier targets for hungry robins.
February 22 is roughly when the moon completes its second full cycle since New Year's Day - that's about 59 days of moon phases!
In Japan, plum trees burst into pink and white flowers around this date, even while snow might still be on the ground - nature's way of showing off!
Here's something wild: February 22 is only about 4 weeks away from the spring equinox, when day and night become exactly equal length everywhere on Earth.
Bears in northern regions are still deep in hibernation on February 22, but their heart rate is starting to speed up as their bodies sense longer days coming.
The air temperature can swing like crazy around this date - it might be 60°F one day and snowing the next because winter and spring are basically arm-wrestling in the atmosphere!
